title: Festive Tangy Soy Tofu Stir-fry
description: A dense and festive Korean dish featuring shaoxing wine and fermented ingredients, this tangy stir-fry is dairy-free and perfect for a savory snack or light meal.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Regular soy sauce is fine, but tamari may be used for a gluten-free option

ingredients.name: Shaoxing Wine
ingredients.notes: Reduces bitterness and adds depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Fermented Black Beans
ingredients.notes: Use 30% less than standard measurements as they are concentrated

ingredients.name: Scallions, thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: Green parts only for color and flavor

ingredients.name: Soy Milk or Coconut Milk
ingredients.notes: For a creamy texture, optional but recommended

instructions: Press tofu to remove excess water and cut into small cubes.
Heat oil in a wok or large frying pan. Add sliced scallions and ginger for aroma.
Add fermented black beans and fry until fragrant and slightly darker.
Drain any excess soy milk, if used, from the tofu cubes. Toss with soy sauce and shaoxing wine. If using chili garlic sauce, add now and stir-fry gently.
Fold in cooked black bean mixture, and mix well to combine all ingredients evenly.
If desired, let the dish cook for an additional minute or two until heated through.
Serve hot as a snack or light meal with steamed vegetables on the side.
